Metadata.CommitMsg:
Merge pull request #2563 from OWASP/copilot/fix-failing-dead-link-check

fix: exclude false-positive and broken links from dead link checker
Metadata.Author: Jeroen Willemsen <jeroenwillemsen2001@gmail.com>

Misconfigurations.Title: No HEALTHCHECK defined
Misconfigurations.Description: You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.
Misconfigurations.Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files
Misconfigurations.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "ARG_BASED_PASSWORD" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files
Misconfigurations.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "AZURE_KEY_VAULT_ENABLED" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files
Misconfigurations.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "DOCKER_ENV_PASSWORD" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files
Misconfigurations.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY" in ARG
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files
Misconfigurations.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files
Misconfigurations.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "WRONGSECRETS_MCP_SECRET" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Title: Image user should not be 'root'
Misconfigurations.Description: Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.
Misconfigurations.Message: Specify at least 1 USER command in Dockerfile with non-root user as argument
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Add 'USER <non root user name>' line to the Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: No HEALTHCHECK defined
Misconfigurations.Description: You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.
Misconfigurations.Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files
Misconfigurations.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "CHALLENGE_RANDO_KEY_CTF_TO_PROVIDE_TO_HOST" in ARG
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files
Misconfigurations.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "CTF_KEY" in ARG
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY" in ARG
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "SPECIAL_K8S_SECRET" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "SPECIAL_SPECIAL_K8S_SECRET" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "challenge_rando_key_ctf_to_provide_to_host_value" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files
Misconfigurations.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
Misconfigurations.Message: Possible exposure of secret env "ctf_key" in ENV
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with UID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with user ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'secret-challenge' of Deployment 'secret-challenge' should set 'securityContext.runAsUser' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with GID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with group ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'secret-challenge' of Deployment 'secret-challenge' should set 'securityContext.runAsGroup' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Workloads in the default namespace
Misconfigurations.Description: Checks whether a workload is running in the default namespace.
Misconfigurations.Message: deployment secret-challenge in default namespace should set metadata.namespace to a non-default namespace

Misconfigurations.Title: Restrict container images to trusted registries
Misconfigurations.Description: Ensure that all containers use images only from trusted registry domains.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container secret-challenge in deployment secret-challenge (namespace: default) uses an image from an untrusted registry.

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets Manager should use customer managed keys
Misconfigurations.Description: Secrets Manager encrypts secrets by default using a default key created by AWS. To ensure control and granularity of secret encryption, CMK's should be used explicitly.
Misconfigurations.Message: Secret explicitly uses the default key.

Misconfigurations.Title: Secrets Manager should use customer managed keys
Misconfigurations.Description: Secrets Manager encrypts secrets by default using a default key created by AWS. To ensure control and granularity of secret encryption, CMK's should be used explicitly.
Misconfigurations.Message: Secret explicitly uses the default key.

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should block public ACL
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should block public ACLs on buckets and any objects they contain. By blocking, PUTs with fail if the object has any public ACL a.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not blocking public acls
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable blocking any PUT calls with a public ACL specified

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should block public policy
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 bucket policy should have block public policy to prevent users from putting a policy that enable public access.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not blocking public policies
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Prevent policies that allow public access being PUT

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Bucket Logging
Misconfigurations.Description: Ensures S3 bucket logging is enabled for S3 buckets
Misconfigurations.Message: Bucket has logging disabled
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Add a logging block to the resource to enable access logging

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Data should be versioned
Misconfigurations.Description:
Versioning in Amazon S3 is a means of keeping multiple variants of an object in the same bucket.

You can use the S3 Versioning feature to preserve, retrieve, and restore every version of every object stored in your buckets.

With versioning you can recover more easily from both unintended user actions and application failures.

When you enable versioning, also keep in mind the potential costs of storing noncurrent versions of objects. To help manage those costs, consider setting up an S3 Lifecycle configuration.
Misconfigurations.Message: Bucket does not have versioning enabled
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable versioning to protect against accidental/malicious removal or modification

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access Block should Ignore Public ACL
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should ignore public ACLs on buckets and any objects they contain. By ignoring rather than blocking, PUT calls with public ACLs will still be applied but the ACL will be ignored.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not blocking public acls
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable ignoring the application of public ACLs in PUT calls

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should restrict public bucket to limit access
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should restrict public policies for the bucket. By enabling, the restrict_public_buckets, only the bucket owner and AWS Services can access if it has a public policy.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not restricting public buckets
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Limit the access to public buckets to only the owner or AWS Services (eg; CloudFront)

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 buckets should each define an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block
Misconfigurations.Description: The "block public access" settings in S3 override individual policies that apply to a given bucket, meaning that all public access can be controlled in one central types for that bucket. It is therefore good practice to define these settings for each bucket in order to clearly define the public access that can be allowed for it.
Misconfigurations.Message: Bucket does not have a corresponding public access block.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Define a aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block for the given bucket to control public access policies

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 encryption should use Customer Managed Keys
Misconfigurations.Description:
Encryption using AWS keys provides protection for your S3 buckets. To gain greater control over encryption, such as key rotation, access policies, and auditability, use customer managed keys (CMKs) with SSE-KMS.
Note that SSE-KMS is not supported for S3 server access logging destination buckets; in such cases, use SSE-S3 instead.
Misconfigurations.Message: Bucket does not encrypt data with a customer managed key.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use SSE-KMS with a customer managed key (CMK)

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure AKS logging to Azure Monitoring is Configured
Misconfigurations.Description: Ensure AKS logging to Azure Monitoring is configured for containers to monitor the performance of workloads.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have logging enabled via OMS Agent.

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure AKS cluster has Network Policy configured
Misconfigurations.Description: The Kubernetes object type NetworkPolicy should be defined to have opportunity allow or block traffic to pods, as in a Kubernetes cluster configured with default settings, all pods can discover and communicate with each other without any restrictions.
Misconfigurations.Message: Kubernetes cluster does not have a network policy set.

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure AKS cluster has private cluster enabled
Misconfigurations.Description: A public AKS API server endpoint increases exposure to unauthorized access or attack. Enable private cluster to ensure the API server endpoint is only accessible from within the virtual network.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have private cluster enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Provision the AKS cluster with `private_cluster_enabled = true` and use private endpoints.

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure AKS cluster has Azure Policy add-on enabled
Misconfigurations.Description: Azure Kubernetes Service should enable Azure Policy Add-On to enforce compliance and governance policies on the cluster. The add-on extends Gatekeeper v3, an admission controller webhook for Open Policy Agent (OPA).
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have Azure Policy add-on enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable Azure Policy add-on on the AKS cluster to enforce governance policies.

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure AKS cluster has disk encryption set ID configured
Misconfigurations.Description: Azure Kubernetes clusters should define a disk encryption set ID to ensure encrypted storage for OS and data disks. This provides an additional layer of security by encrypting data at rest.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have disk encryption set ID configured.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Configure a disk encryption set ID for the AKS cluster to enable customer-managed key encryption.

Misconfigurations.Title: Key vault should have the network acl block specified
Misconfigurations.Description:
Network ACLs allow you to reduce your exposure to risk by limiting what can access your key vault.

The default action of the Network ACL should be set to deny for when IPs are not matched. Azure services can be allowed to bypass.
Misconfigurations.Message: Vault network ACL does not block access by default.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a network ACL for the key vault

Misconfigurations.Title: Key vault Secret should have a content type set
Misconfigurations.Description:
Content Type is an optional Key Vault Secret behavior and is not enabled by default.

Clients may specify the content type of a secret to assist in interpreting the secret data when it's retrieved. The maximum length of this field is 255 characters. There are no pre-defined values. The suggested usage is as a hint for interpreting the secret data.
Misconfigurations.Message: Secret does not have a content-type specified.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Provide content type for secrets to aid interpretation on retrieval

Misconfigurations.Title: Key vault Secret should have a content type set
Misconfigurations.Description:
Content Type is an optional Key Vault Secret behavior and is not enabled by default.

Clients may specify the content type of a secret to assist in interpreting the secret data when it's retrieved. The maximum length of this field is 255 characters. There are no pre-defined values. The suggested usage is as a hint for interpreting the secret data.
Misconfigurations.Message: Secret does not have a content-type specified.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Provide content type for secrets to aid interpretation on retrieval

Misconfigurations.Title: Key vault Secret should have a content type set
Misconfigurations.Description:
Content Type is an optional Key Vault Secret behavior and is not enabled by default.

Clients may specify the content type of a secret to assist in interpreting the secret data when it's retrieved. The maximum length of this field is 255 characters. There are no pre-defined values. The suggested usage is as a hint for interpreting the secret data.
Misconfigurations.Message: Secret does not have a content-type specified.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Provide content type for secrets to aid interpretation on retrieval

Misconfigurations.Title: Key vault should have purge protection enabled
Misconfigurations.Description:
Purge protection is an optional Key Vault behavior and is not enabled by default.

Purge protection can only be enabled once soft-delete is enabled. It can be turned on via CLI or PowerShell.
Misconfigurations.Message: Vault does not have purge protection enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable purge protection for key vaults

Misconfigurations.Title: Key Vault Secret should have an expiration date set
Misconfigurations.Description:
Expiration Date is an optional Key Vault Secret behavior and is not set by default.

Set when the resource will be become inactive.
Misconfigurations.Message: Secret should have an expiry date specified.

Misconfigurations.Title: Key Vault Secret should have an expiration date set
Misconfigurations.Description:
Expiration Date is an optional Key Vault Secret behavior and is not set by default.

Set when the resource will be become inactive.
Misconfigurations.Message: Secret should have an expiry date specified.

Misconfigurations.Title: Key Vault Secret should have an expiration date set
Misconfigurations.Description:
Expiration Date is an optional Key Vault Secret behavior and is not set by default.

Set when the resource will be become inactive.
Misconfigurations.Message: Secret should have an expiry date specified.

Misconfigurations.Title: The default action on Storage account network rules should be set to deny
Misconfigurations.Description:
The default_action for network rules should come into effect when no other rules are matched.
The default action should be set to Deny.
Misconfigurations.Message: No network rules defined and default action allows access.

Misconfigurations.Title: Storage account should have logging enabled
Misconfigurations.Description:
Storage Analytics logs detailed information about successful and failed requests to a storage service.
This information can be used to monitor individual requests and to diagnose issues with a storage service.
Logging should be enabled for at least one storage service type.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account does not have logging enabled for any service.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable logging for at least one storage service (Queue, Table, or Blob)

Misconfigurations.Title: Storage account should use geo-redundant replication
Misconfigurations.Description:
Geo-redundant storage (GRS) replicates your data to a secondary region that is hundreds of miles away from the primary region.
This provides an additional level of durability for your data in the event of a complete regional outage or a disaster.
Options include GRS, RAGRS, GZRS, and RAGZRS for geo-redundancy.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account does not use geo-redundant replication.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Configure storage account to use geo-redundant replication (GRS, RAGRS, GZRS, or RAGZRS)

Misconfigurations.Title: Storage account should use customer-managed keys for encryption
Misconfigurations.Description:
Storage accounts should use customer-managed keys (CMK) for encryption to provide additional control over the encryption keys.
Customer-managed keys allow you to create, rotate, disable, and revoke access controls.
They also provide greater flexibility to audit the encryption keys that are used to protect your data.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account with blob containers should use customer-managed keys for encryption.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Configure customer-managed keys for storage account encryption

Misconfigurations.Title: Storage account should have infrastructure encryption enabled
Misconfigurations.Description:
Infrastructure encryption provides an additional layer of encryption at the infrastructure level.
When infrastructure encryption is enabled, data in the storage account is encrypted twice -
once at the service level and once at the infrastructure level with two different encryption algorithms.
This provides double encryption for enhanced security.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account does not have infrastructure encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable infrastructure encryption for storage account

Misconfigurations.Title: Legacy metadata endpoints enabled.
Misconfigurations.Description: The Compute Engine instance metadata server exposes legacy v0.1 and v1beta1 endpoints, which do not enforce metadata query headers. This is a feature in the v1 APIs that makes it more difficult for a potential attacker to retrieve instance metadata. Unless specifically required, we recommend you disable these legacy APIs. When setting the metadata block, the default value for disable-legacy-endpoints is set to true, they should not be explicitly enabled.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster has legacy metadata endpoints enabled.

Lines.Content: # Google recommends custom service accounts that have cloud-platform scope and permissions granted via IAM Roles.
Lines.Highlighted: [38;5;239m    # Google recommends custom service accounts that have cloud-platform scope and permissions granted via IAM Roles.

Misconfigurations.Title: Clusters should have IP aliasing enabled
Misconfigurations.Description: IP aliasing allows the reuse of public IPs internally, removing the need for a NAT gateway.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster has IP aliasing disabled.

Misconfigurations.Title: Clusters should be configured with Labels
Misconfigurations.Description: Labels make it easier to manage assets and differentiate between clusters and environments, allowing the mapping of computational resources to the wider organisational structure.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not use GCE resource labels.

Misconfigurations.Title: Network Policy should be enabled on GKE clusters
Misconfigurations.Description: Enabling a network policy allows the segregation of network traffic by namespace
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have a network policy enabled.

Misconfigurations.Title: Node metadata value disables metadata concealment.
Misconfigurations.Description: In provider versions prior to 4: The attribute workload_metadata_config.node_metadata configures how node metadata is exposed to workloads. It should be set to SECURE to limit metadata exposure, or GKE_METADATA_SERVER if Workload Identity is enabled. Starting with provider version 4: The attribute node_metadata has been removed. Instead, workload_metadata_configuration.mode controls node metadata exposure. When Workload Identity is enabled, it should be set to GKE_METADATA to prevent unnecessary exposure of the metadata API to workloads.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster exposes node metadata of pools by default.

Lines.Content: # Google recommends custom service accounts that have cloud-platform scope and permissions granted via IAM Roles.
Lines.Highlighted: [38;5;239m    # Google recommends custom service accounts that have cloud-platform scope and permissions granted via IAM Roles.

Misconfigurations.Title: Clusters should be set to private
Misconfigurations.Description: Enabling private nodes on a cluster ensures the nodes are only available internally as they will only be assigned internal addresses.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have private nodes.

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure that Cloud Storage buckets have uniform bucket-level access enabled
Misconfigurations.Description: When you enable uniform bucket-level access on a bucket, Access Control Lists (ACLs) are disabled, and only bucket-level Identity and Access Management (IAM) permissions grant access to that bucket and the objects it contains. You revoke all access granted by object ACLs and the ability to administrate permissions using bucket ACLs.
Misconfigurations.Message: Bucket has uniform bucket level access disabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable uniform bucket level access to provide a uniform permissioning system.

Misconfigurations.Title: Cloud Storage buckets should be encrypted with a customer-managed key.
Misconfigurations.Description: Using unmanaged keys makes rotation and general management difficult.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage bucket encryption does not use a customer-managed key.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Encrypt Cloud Storage buckets using customer-managed keys.

Misconfigurations.Title: Cloud Storage Bucket Logging Not Enabled
Misconfigurations.Description: Cloud Storage bucket access logs should be enabled for audit purposes.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage bucket logging is not configured with a target log bucket.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable Access and Storage logs for Cloud Storage buckets by configuring a log sink or specifying a `log_bucket` in Terraform.

Misconfigurations.Title: VPC flow logs should be enabled for all subnetworks
Misconfigurations.Description: VPC flow logs record information about all traffic, which is a vital tool in reviewing anomalous traffic.
Misconfigurations.Message: Subnetwork does not have VPC flow logs enabled.

Misconfigurations.Title: VPC flow logs should be enabled for all subnetworks
Misconfigurations.Description: VPC flow logs record information about all traffic, which is a vital tool in reviewing anomalous traffic.
Misconfigurations.Message: Subnetwork does not have VPC flow logs enabled.

Misconfigurations.Title: Google Compute Subnetwork with Private Google Access Disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: Private Google Access allows instances in a subnet to reach Google APIs and services via internal IP, which should be enabled for private networks.
Misconfigurations.Message: Subnetwork does not have Private Google Access configured.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable Private Google Access on subnets. In Terraform, set `private_ip_google_access = true` in the `google_compute_subnetwork` resource.

Misconfigurations.Title: Google Compute Subnetwork with Private Google Access Disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: Private Google Access allows instances in a subnet to reach Google APIs and services via internal IP, which should be enabled for private networks.
Misconfigurations.Message: Subnetwork does not have Private Google Access configured.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable Private Google Access on subnets. In Terraform, set `private_ip_google_access = true` in the `google_compute_subnetwork` resource.

Misconfigurations.Title: Google Compute Subnetwork Logging Disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: Flow logs for subnets should be enabled to capture network traffic details for security analysis.
Misconfigurations.Message: Subnetwork does not have flow logs enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable VPC Flow Logs for subnets.

Misconfigurations.Title: Google Compute Subnetwork Logging Disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: Flow logs for subnets should be enabled to capture network traffic details for security analysis.
Misconfigurations.Message: Subnetwork does not have flow logs enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable VPC Flow Logs for subnets.

Misconfigurations.Title: Image user should not be 'root'
Misconfigurations.Description: Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.
Misconfigurations.Message: Specify at least 1 USER command in Dockerfile with non-root user as argument
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Add 'USER <non root user name>' line to the Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: No HEALTHCHECK defined
Misconfigurations.Description: You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.
Misconfigurations.Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: Image user should not be 'root'
Misconfigurations.Description: Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.
Misconfigurations.Message: Specify at least 1 USER command in Dockerfile with non-root user as argument
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Add 'USER <non root user name>' line to the Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: No HEALTHCHECK defined
Misconfigurations.Description: You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.
Misconfigurations.Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: CPU not limited
Misconfigurations.Description: Enforcing CPU limits prevents DoS via resource exhaustion.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'app' of Pod 'secure-pod' should set 'resources.limits.cpu'

Misconfigurations.Title: Root file system is not read-only
Misconfigurations.Description: An immutable root file system prevents applications from writing to their local disk. This can limit intrusions, as attackers will not be able to tamper with the file system or write foreign executables to disk.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'app' of Pod 'secure-pod' should set 'securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to true

Misconfigurations.Title: CPU requests not specified
Misconfigurations.Description: When containers have resource requests specified, the scheduler can make better decisions about which nodes to place pods on, and how to deal with resource contention.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'app' of Pod 'secure-pod' should set 'resources.requests.cpu'

Misconfigurations.Title: Memory requests not specified
Misconfigurations.Description: When containers have memory requests specified, the scheduler can make better decisions about which nodes to place pods on, and how to deal with resource contention.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'app' of Pod 'secure-pod' should set 'resources.requests.memory'

Misconfigurations.Title: Memory not limited
Misconfigurations.Description: Enforcing memory limits prevents DoS via resource exhaustion.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'app' of Pod 'secure-pod' should set 'resources.limits.memory'

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with UID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with user ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'app' of Pod 'secure-pod' should set 'securityContext.runAsUser' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with GID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with group ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'app' of Pod 'secure-pod' should set 'securityContext.runAsGroup' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Runtime/Default Seccomp profile not set
Misconfigurations.Description: According to pod security standard 'Seccomp', the RuntimeDefault seccomp profile must be required, or allow specific additional profiles.
Misconfigurations.Message: Either Pod or Container should set 'securityContext.seccompProfile.type' to 'RuntimeDefault'

Misconfigurations.Title: Seccomp policies disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: A program inside the container can bypass Seccomp protection policies.
Misconfigurations.Message: container "app" of pod "secure-pod" in "default" namespace should specify a seccomp profile
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Specify seccomp either by annotation or by seccomp profile type having allowed values as per pod security standards

Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'sidecar' of Deployment 'secret-challenge-53' should set 'securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to true

Misconfigurations.Title: EKS Clusters should have cluster control plane logging turned on
Misconfigurations.Description: By default cluster control plane logging is not turned on. Logging is available for audit, api, authenticator, controllerManager and scheduler. All logging should be turned on for cluster control plane.
Misconfigurations.Message: Control plane controller manager logging is not enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable logging for the EKS control plane

Misconfigurations.Title: EKS Clusters should have cluster control plane logging turned on
Misconfigurations.Description: By default cluster control plane logging is not turned on. Logging is available for audit, api, authenticator, controllerManager and scheduler. All logging should be turned on for cluster control plane.
Misconfigurations.Message: Control plane scheduler logging is not enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable logging for the EKS control plane

Misconfigurations.Title: EKS Clusters should have the public access disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: EKS clusters are available publicly by default, this should be explicitly disabled in the vpc_config of the EKS cluster resource.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public cluster access is enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Don't enable public access to EKS Clusters

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Misconfigurations.Title: VPC Flow Logs is a feature that enables you to capture information about the IP traffic going to and from network interfaces in your VPC. After you've created a flow log, you can view and retrieve its data in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. It is recommended that VPC Flow Logs be enabled for packet "Rejects" for VPCs.
Misconfigurations.Description: VPC Flow Logs provide visibility into network traffic that traverses the VPC and can be used to detect anomalous traffic or insight during security workflows.
Misconfigurations.Message: VPC does not have VPC Flow Logs enabled.
